compositions.name: DeerFlow
compositions.intent: Turn a single research question into an automated deep-research run by composing a planner, a supervisor that dispatches specialised worker agents, retrieval tools, and a final report writer over a stateful LangGraph graph.
compositions.description: DeerFlow (Deep Exploration and Efficient Research Flow) is ByteDance's MIT-licensed open-source deep-research framework, first released around May 2025. The architecture catalogued here is the classic DeerFlow 1.x graph (the `main-1.x` branch), built on LangGraph and LangChain as a stateful multi-agent workflow. A coordinator node gates incoming requests and routes research questions onward; an optional background-investigator runs a preliminary web search to ground planning; a planner decomposes the question into a typed, multi-step plan; a human-feedback interrupt lets the user accept, edit in natural language, or auto-accept the plan; a research-team node then supervises the plan, dispatching each step to a ReAct researcher (web search, crawling, retrieval, MCP tools) or a coder (Python REPL); and a reporter synthesises the findings into a cited final report. Iteration is bounded by max_plan_iterations and max_step_num. The project has since moved to a separate DeerFlow 2.0 "SuperAgent harness" on the `main` branch (a lead-agent plus dynamic subagents in Docker sandboxes), so the node names and file paths referenced here apply to `main-1.x`.
primary_use_cases: automated deep research over a single natural-language question
long-form, cited report generation from web and private-KB sources
plan-then-execute research with a human plan-review gate
multi-agent workflows combining web search, retrieval, and Python analysis
compositions.agent_loop_shape: LangGraph state graph: coordinator -> (background_investigator) -> planner -> human_feedback -> research_team, which dispatches each plan step to researcher or coder (ReAct loops) and collects results back into shared state; the planner may re-plan up to max_plan_iterations before control passes to reporter, which writes the final report.

members.note: The research_team node is a supervisor that iterates the plan and dispatches each step to specialised worker agents (researcher, analyst, coder), collecting their outputs back into shared state.

evidence.title: README — Research Team
evidence.quote: Research Team: A collection of specialized agents that execute the plan: Researcher ... Coder ...

members.note: The planner node produces an explicit, typed multi-step Plan (validated to a Pydantic model) that the research_team then executes step by step, separating planning from execution.

evidence.title: src/graph/nodes.py — planner_node
evidence.quote: def planner_node(state: State, config: RunnableConfig) -> Command[Literal["human_feedback", "reporter"]]: """Planner node that generate the full plan."""

evidence.title: src/prompts/planner.md — bounded plan
evidence.quote: - **Maximum Steps**: Limit the plan to a maximum of {{ max_step_num }} steps for focused research.

members.note: The human_feedback node interrupts the run to surface the plan; the user can accept it ([ACCEPTED]), edit it in natural language ([EDIT_PLAN], looping back to the planner), or enable auto-accept to skip review.

evidence.title: src/graph/nodes.py — human_feedback_node
evidence.quote: if not auto_accepted_plan: feedback = interrupt("Please Review the Plan.") ... if feedback_normalized.startswith("[EDIT_PLAN]"): ... goto="planner"

evidence.title: README — Human-in-the-loop
evidence.quote: Human-in-the-loop — Supports interactive modification of research plans using natural language; Supports auto-acceptance of research plans.

members.note: The researcher and coder workers are ReAct agents that interleave reasoning with tool calls; they are constructed via a create_react_agent / create_agent factory and bound to search, retrieval, MCP, and Python-REPL tools.

evidence.title: src/agents/agents.py — ReAct agent factory
evidence.quote: from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent ... return create_react_agent(name=agent_name, model=get_llm_by_type(AGENT_LLM_MAP[agent_type]), tools=tools, prompt=lambda state: apply_prompt_template(prompt_template, state),)

members.note: The researcher dynamically attaches web-search tools (Tavily, DuckDuckGo, Brave, Arxiv, Wikipedia) and private knowledge-base retrievers (RAGFlow, VikingDB, Milvus, Qdrant) and reasons over the retrieved content.

evidence.title: src/config/tools.py — SearchEngine enum
evidence.quote: class SearchEngine(enum.Enum): TAVILY = "tavily" ... DUCKDUCKGO = "duckduckgo" ... BRAVE_SEARCH = "brave_search" ... ARXIV = "arxiv"

evidence.title: README — private knowledgebase
evidence.quote: DeerFlow supports private knowledgebase such as RAGFlow, Qdrant, Milvus, and VikingDB.

members.note: The coordinator node is the entry point that classifies the request, handling greetings or small talk with a direct response and handing research questions off to the planner (optionally via background investigation).

evidence.title: src/graph/nodes.py — coordinator_node
evidence.quote: def coordinator_node(state, config) -> Command[Literal["planner", "background_investigator", "coordinator", "__end__"]]: """Coordinator node that communicate with customers and handle clarification."""

evidence.title: README — Coordinator
evidence.quote: Coordinator: The entry point that manages the workflow lifecycle ... Delegates tasks to the planner when appropriate.

members.note: When the planner judges that context is insufficient it re-plans, bounded by max_plan_iterations; once the cap is reached control passes to the reporter rather than looping indefinitely.

evidence.title: src/config/configuration.py — iteration bounds
evidence.quote: max_plan_iterations: int = 1  # Maximum number of plan iterations / max_step_num: int = 3  # Maximum number of steps in a plan

members.note: Worker agents can be extended with external tools via the Model Context Protocol; configured MCP servers are loaded through MultiServerMCPClient and their tools merged into the agent's tool set at runtime.

evidence.title: src/graph/nodes.py — MCP client setup
evidence.quote: if mcp_servers: client = MultiServerMCPClient(mcp_servers) all_tools = await client.get_tools()

evidence.title: README — MCP Seamless Integration
evidence.quote: MCP Seamless Integration — Expand capabilities for private domain access, knowledge graph, web browsing and more.
